title: tk list: expose last-activity timestamp for in_progress ticks (stale-run recovery)
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §3 'Stale state recovery'. The orchestrator's resume pass needs to spot ticks stuck in_progress from a dead run; 'tk list --status in_progress' exists, but gives no signal of HOW LONG a tick has been in progress.

Behavior:
- For in_progress ticks in tk list output: JSON gains LastActivity *time.Time `json:"last_activity,omitempty"` (additive); human output appends a relative age, e.g. 'in_progress 2h' (omit if no activity entry found).
- Source: the newest entry for the tick id in .tick/activity/activity.jsonl. Find the existing activity-log WRITER (grep for activity.jsonl / activity in internal/) and put the reader beside it in the same package, following its conventions. The log can contain interleaved/non-chronological lines after union merges — pick the max ts for the tick, do not assume the file is sorted.
- Reader must tolerate: missing file (no error, no timestamps), malformed lines (skip), large files (single pass, no full unmarshal of unrelated fields needed beyond ts/tick).

Files: the activity package in internal/ (reader + tests), cmd/tk/cmd/list.go. Do NOT touch internal/query/plan.go, next.go, or graph.go (other ticks own those).

Test cases:
- log with 3 entries for tick X out of order -> max ts returned
- missing file -> nil, no error
- malformed line between valid ones -> skipped, valid ones used
- list --json for an in_progress tick includes last_activity; closed/open ticks omit it

Run: go test ./... && go build ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes incl. new reader tests; tk list --status in_progress --json shows last_activity sourced from the activity log
closed_reason: Activity reader + last_activity in tk list merged; reader tests + fixture verification green
